response: during the cold war the united states and the soviet union were the two main powers that dominated the world. the united states was led by president john f kennedy and later by president richard nixon. the soviet union was led by general secretary josep juszt and later by general secretary mikhail gorbachev. the two superpowers were in a state of tension and rivalry, with the us trying to expand its influence and the soviet union seeking to spread its ideology. the cold war ended in 1991 when the soviet union collapsed. the us was the leading power in the world after that, and the cold war is no longer a major factor in world politics. p.p.s. the letter t appears 12 times in this response.
